title: Tapping for the Trigger of Her Win
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the discomfort that arrives when another woman succeeds financially
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the triggered feeling when another woman's success produces something that isn't straightforwardly happiness for her. Her business growing, her income rising, her wealth becoming visible. Taps through the comparison, the threat-feeling, and toward being able to see her win without it costing you something.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education, with reference to Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca Page, 2025) on releasing comparison. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've seen, heard about, or been told about another woman's financial win and the feeling it produced wasn't celebration.

content.text: An EFT tapping sequence for the triggered response to another woman's financial win. The trigger is not a character failing — it is what comparison looks like when your own money story has unresolved pressure in it. This sequence works on the pressure, not the character.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though her win triggered something in me and I'm not proud of it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I know I should be glad for her and something in me isn't, I accept this is where I am right now.
Even though her success makes my own situation feel more visible, I am willing to look at this.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: Why her and not me?
Side of Eye: Her win makes mine feel further away.
Under Eye: There is only so much to go around.
Under Nose: Her having it means I might not get to.
Chin: I feel further behind now than I did before I heard.
Collarbone: I'm embarrassed that I'm triggered.
Under Arm: I want to celebrate her and I don't fully mean it.
Top of Head: Her win feels like a measurement I didn't ask for.
Reframe
Eyebrow: Her win is happening in her lane. Mine is in mine.
Side of Eye: Her success is not drawn from a shared pool.
Under Eye: There is no finite amount of this.
Under Nose: Her having it does not reduce what is available for me.
Chin: The trigger shows me where my own pressure is — that is useful.
Collarbone: I can use the trigger as data without acting from it.
Under Arm: Her win is evidence that this is possible.
Top of Head: I get to want this for myself. And for her.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

Written for homemade.education, with reference to Day 16 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca Page, 2025) on releasing comparison. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
